name: Human secretin
description: Human secretin is a peptide hormone that regulated the secretions in the stomach, pancreas, and liver and overall water homeostasis in the body. The hormone is produced from the S cells in the duodenum to regulate the pH of the duodenum. It inhibits the secretion of gastric acid from the parietal cells of the stomach and stimulates the production of bicarbonate from the centroacinar cells and intercalated ducts of the pancreas, in addition to stimulating bile production by the liver. Human secretin is initially stored as the precursor prosecretin which is then activated through the contact with the gastric acid in the lower intestine. The synthetic human secretin is available as an intravenous injection under the market name ChiRhoStim that contains an amino acid sequence identical to the naturally occurring hormone consisting of 27 amino acids. It is indicated for the stimulation of the pancreatic and gastric secretions to aid in the diagnosis of pancreatic exocrine dysfunction and the diagnosis of gastrinoma, and facilitate the identification of the ampulla of Vater and accessory papilla during endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP).
mechanismOfAction: RG1068 is a synthetic human secretin, to improve the assessment of pancreatic duct structures by magnetic resonance imaging (MRI). Secretin is a gastrointestinal hormone involved in the process of digestion. It signals the release of fluids into the ducts of the pancreas, a result that has been documented in the literature to improve MRI imaging of the pancreas.
